summary: Comprehensive testing of SaaS multi-tenancy features: Landing page, Registration, Login (email+username), Super Admin panel, Upgrade page, /auth/me subscription info, and tenant data isolation. All 14 specified features pass. 35/35 backend tests pass, all frontend flows verified.

minor.issue: FastAPI returns 403 for missing auth token instead of HTTP standard 401. This is a FastAPI HTTPBearer behavior but deviates from HTTP standard where 401 = unauthenticated, 403 = unauthorized.

integration_issues.issue: SuperAdminPage component makes API calls first, gets 403, then redirects to /dashboard. The frontend `is_super_admin` check renders 'Access Denied' UI, but the API 403 error handler in the load() callback calls `navigate('/dashboard')` before the user sees the Access Denied message. Functionally the access is blocked correctly, but UX could be improved by checking is_super_admin before API calls.

issues: HTML hydration warnings in browser console: COMPETITORS.map() inside <table> renders <span> wrappers around <th> and <tr> elements, causing invalid HTML nesting (<th> cannot be child of <span>, <span> cannot be child of <tr>, <span> cannot be child of <tbody>). This doesn't break visual rendering but logs React hydration errors. Fix: Use React.Fragment key pattern or renderToString-safe approach.

action_items: Fix HTML hydration warning in LandingPage.js COMPETITORS.map() - use <React.Fragment key={c.name}><th>...</th></React.Fragment> pattern instead of bare array map in table header to avoid invalid <span> wrapper around <th>/<tr>/<tbody> children
Optional: Move `if (!user?.is_super_admin)` check above API calls in SuperAdminPage to show 'Access Denied' UI immediately rather than redirecting to dashboard after 403 API response
critical_code_review_comments: LandingPage.js line 255: `{COMPETITORS.map(c => (<th key={c.name} ...>...))}` inside a `<tr>` - React wraps dynamic arrays with a <span> element causing invalid HTML nesting in tables. Use Fragment key pattern: `{COMPETITORS.map(c => (<React.Fragment key={c.name}><th ...>...</th></React.Fragment>))}`. Same issue in <tbody> rows at line 273.

SuperAdminPage.js load() callback: error handler only catches 403, then navigates to /dashboard. The frontend `if (!user?.is_super_admin)` check at line 49 fires after the component renders, causing a brief redirect behavior instead of immediate Access Denied display.

Backend auth.py returns 403 via FastAPI HTTPBearer for unauthenticated requests. Standard HTTP says 401 for unauthenticated, 403 for unauthorized. Consider using HTTPBearer(auto_error=False) and manually raising 401 for missing tokens.
TenantDB context isolation works correctly: new org users see 0 branches and 0 products (verified by backend tests).
seed_data_creation: Multiple test organizations created during testing (New Company Ltd, Duplicate Company, Trial Test Corp, Test Company, First Company, UI Test Company, etc.) - all visible in superadmin panel.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All SaaS multi-tenancy features work: landing page, registration, login (email+username), superadmin, upgrade page, tenant isolation. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_saas_multitenancy.py (35 tests all pass). The only issues are minor: HTML hydration warnings in LandingPage.js compare table and FastAPI returning 403 instead of 401 for unauthenticated requests. All existing app features (dashboard, branches, products, etc.) still work correctly after multi-tenancy migration.

html_hydration_warning.root_cause: React's array .map() in table contexts wraps rendered elements with a <span data-ve-dynamic> container, causing invalid HTML nesting (<th> cannot be child of <span>, etc.)
html_hydration_warning.location: LandingPage.js lines 255-260 (COMPETITORS.map in thead tr) and lines 273-293 (compare rows in tbody)
html_hydration_warning.fix: Use <React.Fragment key={...}> wrapper around each mapped table element

superadmin_access_redirect.root_cause: SuperAdminPage load() makes 2 API calls on mount. Error handler catches 403 and calls navigate('/dashboard'). The `if (!user?.is_super_admin)` render check at line 49 would show Access Denied but the navigate() in error handler preempts it.
superadmin_access_redirect.fix: Add early return with redirect before API calls: `if (!user?.is_super_admin) { navigate('/dashboard'); return; }` in the useEffect that calls load()
